interfaces.name: Position
interfaces.signature: struct Position: Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let row: Int; let column: Int }
interfaces.notes: Zero-indexed grid coordinate; used by board, threads, and gesture/tap adapters alike.

interfaces.name: ThreadColor
interfaces.signature: enum ThreadColor: String, CaseIterable, Codable, Sendable { case madderRed, indigo, walnutBrown, sage, ochre; var capShape: CapShape }
interfaces.notes: Max 5 per puzzle. capShape gives the fixed colorblind-safe shape mapping from design_handoff (circle/diamond/square/triangle/hexagon).

interfaces.name: CapShape
interfaces.signature: enum CapShape: String, Codable, Sendable { case circle, diamond, square, triangle, hexagon }
interfaces.notes: Secondary non-color identity channel, rendered on thread endpoints and used in VoiceOver labels.

interfaces.name: Direction
interfaces.signature: enum Direction: String, Codable, Sendable { case up, down, left, right }
interfaces.notes: Used to describe segment entry/exit for rendering and for adjacency legality checks.

interfaces.name: CellContent
interfaces.signature: enum CellContent: Codable, Sendable { case empty; case threadEnd(color: ThreadColor); case threadSegment(color: ThreadColor, from: Direction, to: Direction) }
interfaces.notes: One value per board cell; drives both Canvas rendering and the CellAccessibilityElement label text.

interfaces.name: Thread
interfaces.signature: struct Thread: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let color: ThreadColor; var path: [Position]; var isComplete: Bool }
interfaces.notes: path holds only committed cells, per the cell-boundary-commit rule; nothing mid-gesture is ever stored here.

interfaces.name: PuzzleDifficultyTier
interfaces.signature: enum PuzzleDifficultyTier: String, CaseIterable, Codable, Sendable { case gentle, standard; var dimensions: Int }
interfaces.notes: gentle.dimensions == 5, standard.dimensions == 7, per the 44pt-hit-target grid math locked in design_handoff.

interfaces.name: PuzzleBoard
interfaces.signature: struct PuzzleBoard: Codable, Sendable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; let tier: PuzzleDifficultyTier; var cells: [[CellContent]]; var threads: [Thread]; let seed: UInt64; var isFallback: Bool }
interfaces.notes: The materialized puzzle; seed is provenance-only metadata, never used to reconstruct state on resume, per detailed_discussion.

interfaces.name: PuzzleSummary
interfaces.signature: struct PuzzleSummary: Codable, Sendable { let solvedAt: Date; let moveCount: Int; let tier: PuzzleDifficultyTier }
interfaces.notes: Backs Hub's 'yesterday's loom' row; nil means the first-launch empty-state copy is shown.

interfaces.name: PuzzleRecord
interfaces.signature: @Model final class PuzzleRecord { var id: UUID; var tierRaw: String; var createdAt: Date; var solvedAt: Date?; var moveCount: Int; var boardData: Data; var isCurrent: Bool }
interfaces.notes: boardData is a versioned-encoded PuzzleBoard blob. No unique constraints, all optional/defaulted fields, per the CloudKit-compatibility tax locked in ios_architecture_review.

interfaces.name: Entitlement
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Entitlement { var hasLoomPass: Bool; var updatedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Local-only in this slice; shaped so a future StoreKit 2 Transaction.currentEntitlements listener can set it without restructuring.

interfaces.name: PuzzleGenerating
interfaces.signature: protocol PuzzleGenerating: Sendable { func generate(tier: PuzzleDifficultyTier) async throws -> PuzzleBoard }
interfaces.notes: Protocol boundary exists specifically so the 500-run-per-tier solvability test and the future custom-generator panel both target this, not a concrete type.

interfaces.name: PuzzleGeneratorEngine
interfaces.signature: actor PuzzleGeneratorEngine: PuzzleGenerating { func generate(tier: PuzzleDifficultyTier) async throws -> PuzzleBoard }
interfaces.notes: Solve-path-first then strip-to-endpoints. Actor-isolated so calling it from a @MainActor view model via Task { await } genuinely hops off main, per ios_architecture_review.

interfaces.name: GeneratorError
interfaces.signature: enum GeneratorError: Error, Sendable { case timedOut, unableToConverge }
interfaces.notes: Only checked error in the app's domain logic; caught by PuzzleViewModel to transition into .failed(fallback:).

interfaces.name: FallbackPuzzleProvider
interfaces.signature: struct FallbackPuzzleProvider { static func bundledFallback(tier: PuzzleDifficultyTier) -> PuzzleBoard }
interfaces.notes: One precomputed, solvability-verified backup board per tier, bundled at build time; never throws.

interfaces.name: SwiftDataStoring
interfaces.signature: protocol SwiftDataStoring: Sendable { func loadCurrentPuzzle() async -> PuzzleBoard?; func saveCurrentPuzzle(_ board: PuzzleBoard) async; func flushPendingWrites() async; func markSolved(moveCount: Int) async; func loadYesterdaySummary() async -> PuzzleSummary?; func loadEntitlement() async -> Entitlement; func resetAllData() async }
interfaces.notes: Backed by a @ModelActor implementation; coalesces writes with a short debounce and flushes unconditionally on scenePhase backgrounding.

interfaces.name: ThreadTraceController
interfaces.signature: struct ThreadTraceController: Sendable { func commitStep(activeColor: ThreadColor?, from: Position?, to candidate: Position, on board: PuzzleBoard) -> StepResult }
interfaces.notes: The single funnel both DragGesture translation and VoiceOver 'extend thread here' custom actions call into; guarantees the two input modalities can never diverge in legality/retraction behavior.

interfaces.name: StepResult
interfaces.signature: enum StepResult: Sendable { case committed(board: PuzzleBoard); case retracted(otherColor: ThreadColor, board: PuzzleBoard); case rejected }
interfaces.notes: rejected means the gesture continues with no crash and no state change, per the never-block requirement.

interfaces.name: PuzzleScreenState
interfaces.signature: enum PuzzleScreenState: Sendable { case generating; case ready(PuzzleBoard); case solved(PuzzleBoard); case failed(fallback: PuzzleBoard) }
interfaces.notes: The four locked puzzle-screen states; active-trace-in-progress is represented as .ready with a non-empty in-flight thread, not a fifth case.

interfaces.name: PuzzleViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class PuzzleViewModel { var state: PuzzleScreenState; func onAppear() async; func beginThread(at: Position); func extendThread(to: Position); func cancelActiveThread(); func undo(); func beginNextLoom() async }
interfaces.notes: undo() removes the most recently completed thread in full (thread granularity, not cell or drag-frame), per app_features.

interfaces.name: HubViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class HubViewModel { var yesterdaySummary: PuzzleSummary?; var loomPassComingSoonPresented: Bool; func onAppear() async; func loomPassTeaserTapped() }
interfaces.notes: yesterdaySummary == nil renders the first-launch 'your first loom is on the frame now' copy locked in design_handoff, never an omitted row.

interfaces.name: SettingsViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class SettingsViewModel { var isResetConfirmationPresented: Bool; func requestReset(); func confirmReset() async }
interfaces.notes: confirmReset() drives the same ResetConfirmationView instance reused for corrupted-store recovery at launch.

interfaces.name: HapticsPlaying
interfaces.signature: protocol HapticsPlaying: Sendable { func playThreadCommit(); func playSolveBloom() }
interfaces.notes: Protocol so tests can inject a no-op fake; the Core Haptics-backed implementation is only verifiable on a physical device.

interfaces.name: recoverFromCorruptStore
interfaces.signature: func recoverFromCorruptStore(at url: URL) throws -> ModelContainer
interfaces.notes: Called if ModelContainer init throws at launch; deletes the unreadable store and returns a fresh empty container, surfaced via the shared reset/starting-fresh screen.
